High above this rust-bleached floor, the twin suns hang like two hot brass coins dropped into a well of frozen ink, their competing glare cutting the thin atmosphere into sharp, bleeding ribbons of violet and cadmium yellow. To look down is to witness the fragile, geodesic domes of the settlements clinging to the crater lips like grey fish-scales on a rotting carcass, their small, flickering lithium beacons the only proof of a species trying to breathe where God had intended only stone and silence.

From this high, freezing shelf, {given_name} watches the green phosphor of the tactical displays crawl across the glass, superimposing columns of ore yield and infant mortality directly over the stark, real-world horizon. The red-shifted glare of the main telemetry console throws long, skeletal shadows across the riveted iron bulkheads, turning the tiny command deck into a chapel of cold numbers and oil-starved fans. Every twitch of the stylus in {full_name}’s hand is a stroke that could starve three hundred families in the outer ring or turn the red silt below into a private, sovereign fiefdom. The raw, unfiltered glare of the stellar expanse makes the old parchment maps of Earth seem like small, faded toys; the true frontier is this wide, unblinking dark where the human eye finds no boundary except the limit of its own hunger.

The navigation deck is suddenly flooded with the harsh, flickering data-scrawl of a rogue scout's log: a raw, unmonitored corridor of hyper-lanes leading into the deep dark, offering a desperate, violent leap into the unmapped cluster before the old nations can draft their treaties.

choice.text: Advocate for rapid, unrestrained interstellar colonization.

prose: Against this feverish hunger, the senior surveyor presents a sober, monochrome ledger of the current terraforming failures, arguing that to plunge further into the void without securing the soil beneath their boots is to invite a quiet, frozen death for every soul already here.

choice.text: Endorse a controlled, sustainable expansion model.

decision.prompt: How will our family carve its legacy into the stars – through domination, cooperation, or self-sufficiency?

options.text: Enforce Earth-centric corporate and political control over new colonies, ensuring loyalty and profit.
Foster an independent, cooperative stellar federation, advocating for self-governance and shared prosperity.
Prioritize our family's independent expansion and self-sufficiency, building an autonomous dominion beyond external control.

prose: The grey nutrient paste, drawn from the communal brass-headed dispenser, coats the tongue with a sterile, chalky sweetness—a thin imitation of wild orchid vanilla that lacks the warm, fat richness of the soils {given_name} once read of in the ancestral journals. It is a flavor engineered for survival rather than joy, accompanied by the flat, twice-recycled moisture of the habitat's atmospheric scrubbers, which always leaves the faint, copper-penny tang of ionized radiation on the back of the teeth. To consume it is to swallow the reality of the frontier itself: a lean, metallic existence where even nourishment is a calculated transaction with the void, stripped of indulgence but hardened into absolute utility.

Yet, the swallow of ceremonial synth-wine that follows provides a different, sharper heat, its artificial tannins mimicking the dry, volcanic ash of lost Mediterranean slopes before dissolving into the clinical burn of high-grade laboratory ethanol. It is a bittersweet vintage of necessity and memory, leaving a dark, lingering aftertaste of dried plum and scorched iron that clings to the palate long after the glass is set down. For {full_name}, this is the very flavor of their legacy—a draft brewed from ancient dreams and cold, vacuum-sealed realities, a vintage of blood-earned dominion that their heir must now learn to stomach, tolerate, and ultimately command.
As the generation closes, you survey the vast network of colonies and resource lines, a tangible testament to your family's expansion.

choice.text: Emphasize the material wealth and territorial gains to your heir.

prose: You reflect on the political infrastructure and alliances forged, the diplomatic triumphs that secured your family's standing among the stars.

choice.text: Highlight the political influence and governance structures to your heir.

decision.prompt: As the copper tang of the recycled air settles on your tongue, the colony administrator's proposal sits on the cold metal desk between you. How will the {family_name} name endure this sterile frontier?

options.text: We must bind our name to the hydroponics director's family; though the contract is as dry as the habitat's dust, our shared quarters will host children who will one day taste real soil.
I will raise my sister's orphaned child as my sole heir, bypassing the theater of a political marriage to focus every scrap of our remaining influence on securing our seat on the high council.

Let the {surname} line end with my own breath; I will pour our remaining wealth and political leverage into the terraforming initiative, ensuring that even if my blood stops, humanity finally breathes clean air.
